title: Tapping for the Partner Loss
subtitle: A tapping practice for the grief of losing a life partner
excerpt: A tapping practice for the specific grief of losing a life partner. Addresses the size of the loss, the loss of identity within it, and the task of learning to live inside a grief this large.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. The EFT tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique, developed in the mid-1990s.

practice.whenToUse: Use when the grief of losing a partner is present and needs acknowledgement. Good in the early months when the loss is most raw, and in quieter stretches when it resurfaces.

content.text: A tapping practice for the grief of losing a life partner. It addresses the size of the loss, the loss of the self that existed within the relationship, and the task of learning to hold all of it.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop (repeat x3)
Even though I have lost the person I built my life with, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though this grief is bigger than I knew grief could be, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I do not know who I am without them, I am open to finding out, and I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
**Eyebrow:** They are gone.
**Side of eye:** The person I shared my life with is gone.
**Under eye:** This grief is bigger than I knew grief could be.
**Under nose:** Everything reminds me of them.
**Chin:** I do not know how to be a person without them.
**Collarbone:** I carry them everywhere I go.
**Under arm:** The loss is in the bed, in the kitchen, and in the quiet.
**Top of head:** I am learning to live inside a loss this large.
Reframe to positive
**Eyebrow:** I loved them. That love is still real.
**Side of eye:** The love does not end because they are gone.
**Under eye:** I carry them in a different way now.
**Under nose:** I am allowed to grieve as long as I need to.
**Chin:** My grief is evidence of what we had.
**Collarbone:** I am finding my way through this, one day at a time.
**Under arm:** I do not have to have it figured out.
**Top of head:** I am still here. That is enough for today.
Tap the round three times. Then tap the reframe three times.
Where this practice comes from
Original to homemade.education. EFT tapping was developed by Gary Craig in the 1990s.
